seoTitle: Make an Image Transparent in Canva (Quick Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to make an image transparent in Canva using the opacity slider, so you can layer photos and text cleanly in any design.
intro: Adjusting image opacity in Canva lets you blend photos into backgrounds, soften visuals behind text, and layer elements without them overpowering the rest of your design. It's a small adjustment that makes a big difference in product mockups, social posts, and presentations. This guide walks through finding the transparency control and using it effectively.

steps.heading: Select the image on your canvas
steps.body: Click the photo you want to adjust so its editing toolbar appears above it.

steps.heading: Open the transparency control
steps.body: Look for the transparency icon in the top toolbar — it's usually shown as a checkered square.

steps.heading: Drag the opacity slider
steps.body: Move the slider left to reduce opacity and increase transparency; watch the canvas update in real time so you can stop at the right level.

steps.heading: Layer text or other elements on top
steps.body: With the image now more transparent, add text or shapes over it and check that everything stays readable.

steps.heading: Fine-tune and save
steps.body: Adjust the opacity again if needed, then download or continue editing your design once it looks right.

faq.question: Can I make only part of an image transparent in Canva?
faq.answer: Canva's basic opacity slider affects the whole image at once; for partial transparency you'd combine it with an eraser or a frame/mask.

faq.question: Do I need Canva Pro to adjust transparency?
faq.answer: No, the opacity slider is available on Canva's free plan for most standard image elements.

faq.question: Will transparency change how the image prints?
faq.answer: Yes — a transparent image will show whatever is behind it, so check your background before exporting for print.

faq.question: Can I undo the transparency change?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag the slider back to 100% or use Canva's undo button at any time.
